evals.prompt: I'm adding Stripe payments to my Python service. It's laid out as domain/, adapters/, and main.py. Where does the Stripe SDK client go, and is there anything that belongs in domain/ instead?
evals.expected_output: Stripe client -> adapters (infra); domain declares a payment port/interface (e.g. PaymentGateway) plus the charge use-case; main.py constructs the Stripe adapter and injects it. domain imports no stripe SDK.

evals.prompt: Add a feature that fetches the current USD->BRL exchange rate from an external API and converts an Order's total. The Order and its pricing rules live in core/. Show me where each piece goes.
evals.expected_output: core declares a RateProvider port and the conversion use-case (pure, testable); infra implements RateProvider with the HTTP call; run/composition injects it. The HTTP library is never imported in core; the decision and computation stay in core.

evals.prompt: Here's my repo structure (app/, infra/, run/). I think my core might have leaked some I/O. How do I check whether app/ is actually pure, and fix it if not?
evals.expected_output: Run a static purity check over app/ forbidding infra/run imports and I/O modules (the bundled check_core_purity.py), identify offenders, and for each move the I/O behind a port in app implemented in infra; add the check to CI so it can't regress.
